title: T5
description: An encoder-decoder transformer that frames every NLP task as text-to-text generation.
openingSummary: T5 (Text-to-Text Transfer Transformer) is a transformer model family that reads text and writes text, using an encoder to build context and a decoder to generate the answer in the same token space.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: T5 is an encoder-decoder transformer model family from Google Research. It treats every natural language task as text-to-text: the model reads a text prompt and produces a text completion.

inputsAndOutputs.title: Inputs And Outputs
inputsAndOutputs.body: T5 reads tokenized text as input and produces tokenized text as output. Both sides use the same vocabulary, so tasks like translation, summarization, and classification are expressed as prompt-and-answer text pairs.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.body: T5 follows the encoder-decoder transformer layout. An encoder stack processes the full input sequence with bidirectional self-attention, then a decoder stack generates output tokens autoregressively while attending to encoder states through cross-attention.

importantModules.title: Important Modules
importantModules.body: The modules below are the main building blocks that define how T5 processes text. Relative position bias replaces absolute position embeddings, multi-head attention moves information across tokens, cross-attention connects encoder context to the decoder, and the feed-forward network transforms each position locally.

training.title: Training
training.body: T5 is pretrained with a denoising objective on large text corpora, then fine-tuned on downstream tasks expressed as text-to-text examples. The same model weights can be adapted to many tasks by changing only the input and target text formatting.

practicalNotes.title: Practical Notes
practicalNotes.body: T5 remains a widely used reference for encoder-decoder transformers and transfer learning with a unified text interface. Different checkpoint sizes trade off quality and compute, and task-specific prefixes or templates are still needed to steer behavior at inference time.

architectureGraph.alt: T5 architecture diagram with input text, an encoder stack with relative position bias and self-attention, cross-attention into a decoder stack, and output text generation.
